intro: Let me begin this inane exercise with a caveat: If Bill Belichick reunites with Jimmy Garoppolo today, then things could obviously go in a different direction this evening when Roger Goodell begins to flex his bro-hug muscles. Actually, let me clarify: In full disclosure, this entire process is filled with caveats even in the best of times. Even fuller disclosure: In terms of medical information and having a combine clearinghouse for on-field and health testing, this is not the best of times to be mocking 32 players to 32 teams. description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Patriots, Washington trade up for QBs, first eight picks are on offense : This is it, our final mock of the 2021 draft season

And while the name at the top -- Trevor Lawrence -- hasn't changed in 51 weeks, there has certainly been some dramatic turns, whether it's the emergence of Zach Wilson and Mac Jones, or the media perception that Justin Fields has inexplicably slipped. We'll all find out together on Thursday night but since we have a few hours to kill until then, enjoy this one last time. (Until next week, of course, when I'll be unveiling my first mock draft of the 2022 draft season -- and we wish we were making this up.)

The drama, the trades, the shocker of a team valuing one player so differently than the rest of the league. Round after round of surprises. Unless, of course, if you choose to read this mock draft, then you will know how the first four rounds are likely to go. Because when has an NFL mock draft ever been wrong?

We don’t know what’s going to happen this weekend. We may as well have some fun along the way.

intro: After months of speculation the NFL Draft finally begins Thursday night and the dominant story remains how many quarterbacks will go in the top 10. Trevor Lawrence has always been a lock for the No. 1 pick, but when private workouts started things got pushed through the moon. Zach Wilson moved up to be in the top 5 of most mocks, then to No. 2 overall, Trey Lance went from being a likely pick in the 20s to another top 10 pick, and Mac Jones, once seen as a 3rd round pick, shot up boards due to his NFL readiness.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is upon us and we may be in store for one of the most unpredictable drafts in recent memory. With scouts not being able to take college visits, no combine, limited access at Pro Days and no top 30 visits it makes there less group think and everything less predictable. Expect to see some surprises. With that being said here is a look at my final 2021 NFL Mock Draft as I try to predict the first round as accurate as I can. I did figure in some trades but didn’t move teams around much, enjoy!

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft
intro: After months of projecting mocks leading up to the final report it is clear this year’s draft will be like no other in the history of the league.  First, the potential for 5 QB’s and no defensive players taken in the first 10 picks is extremely high. Secondly, Kyle Pitts may be the most coveted player in this draft as a Tight End. Making it very likely that a TE may be drafted in the top 5 picks for the first time since 1972 when the Denver Broncos selected Riley Odoms at #5 overall. Thirdly, there may be three players whose fathers played in the NFL that could be drafted in the first 25 picks (Patrick Surtain II, Jaycee Horn, and Asante Samuel). In my opinion for what it is worth, this first round will be a tale of two halves. The top half of the draft will be overloaded by offensive players. The bottom half of the draft will be dominated by players on the defensive side of the ball.

Could George Paton grab a quarterback? Perhaps, but the one he likes has to be available. What follows is my best guess at tonight’s draft selections if Atlanta decides to take the heir to Matt Ryan. description: Mock Draft 5.0: Six First-Round QBs; Three Projected Trades : The Panthers and Bears trade up for QBs, the Patriots trade for Jimmy Garoppolo and the 49ers choose Mac Jones in our final mock on draft day.
intro: My annual crack at the mock draft is normally a one-time deal. This does not mean I slam it down like a holy tome or attempt to cozy up to the legacy of our great mock draft godfather, Paul Zimmerman. Not even close (and I’d have to get more than five right in a given season to start feeling that way). For me, handling the mock draft this way simply reflects the way you can fall in love with a certain way things could go. Based on some phone calls and intuition, you end up slotting people in a certain place and creating a version of the universe that fits neatly together. But as a wise former coworker once reminded me, that universe is only in one place: the brain of the author.

But in our final rendition, we called an audible at the line, put on our mind-reading caps and made the picks based on what we think will happen.

description: Mock Draft

This, of course, is a mock as to what I believe will unfold on draft night, which believe it or not, or what Kyle Shanahan tells you, will eventually get here. We know the first two picks (or are at least 99% positive Zach Wilson will land with the Jets at No. 2). After that? Mass chaos.

intro: You may ask yourself why my final mock draft of 2021 leads with a Metallica reference. The light in question was the pre-draft process — even in this weird and unpresented year of COVID opt-outs, no scouting combine, and Zoom pressers and pro days when there used to be more boots on the ground, teams were putting their draft boards together and trying to figure out which players best fit their paradigms.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: 49ers go with Justin Fields, teams trade up for Trey Lance and Mac Jones : Will Brinson's only mock draft of 2021 features a surprise at No. 3
intro: To paraphrase Neil Diamond -- I'm only going to do one mock, but I'm gonna do it good. There's a couple key things about this draft you need to understand. First, I have no idea why this is my first mock draft. I guess be glad you're spared me trading some random veteran quarterback in March. Second, everyone is understandably spending their time talking about No. 3 (more on that in a second) but this draft takes a swan dive around 12 or 13. This is why Atlanta can't trade out of No. 4. No one will pay the price to move up to get a quarterback. Part of the problem is Atlanta doesn't want to drop below 12 or so. Also, there's no one willing to pay to come up to the fourth pick relative to what Atlanta wants for the pick. They're probably going to stick there and take Kyle Pitts and they're probably going to look like geniuses for doing it. If you're not sure ... just draft Kyle Pitts.

description: 2021 Acme Packing Company Writers’ Mock Draft : Green Bay finds help at receiver and cornerback in this mock, compiled by nine of APC’s contributors.
intro: The first night of the 2021 NFL Draft is almost upon us. On Thursday evening, the NFL will take over downtown Cleveland, Ohio for the annual selection meeting, which will run through Saturday. But as the draft approaches, so too does the end of mock drafts for this year. Of course, the last few days feature a flurry of mocks as a sort of countdown to the draft’s official start, and today is our last contribution to that countdown. As Acme Packing Company’s final entry into the mock draft universe for 2021, we now present to you our 2021 Writers’ Mock Draft. As has been the case in past years, we assigned each NFL team’s picks to one of several APC contributors (nine in total this year) to make the selections. We worked our way through the first two rounds, encompassing 64 picks, and were able to factor in the weekend trade involving the Baltimore Ravens and Kansas City Chiefs into the mix.

intro: So let's see...this draft will have encompassed a year long pandemic, players, both professional and collegiate that opted not to play, no NFL combine to evaluate players, several new head coaches and general managers, along with the usual injuries and mishaps that occur in any given year etc... With quarterbacks expected to be selected consecutively over the first three selections for the first time since 1999, that sets up for an interesting follow up after that point. Let’s dive into it.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft : You’ve read all the mocks and know how much went into each one. Painstakingly pieced together by ins...
intro: You’ve read all the mocks and know how much went into each one. Painstakingly pieced together by insider reports and talent evaluators, the hairs are barely split between two, three, four options at each pick in the pursuit of accurately projecting the NFL’s most absurd event. What if.. .instead of all that logic, that finagling, that sourcing… you got absolutely none of it? The TDN Mock Draft Machine doesn’t talk to sources or weigh team trends. It just knows what the teams need and how the players are ranked, and with that information, it chugs away at possible first-round pairings. But the MDM still is competitive against TDN’s best mockers -- and the best mockers in the industry altogether.

Phil Perry's final NFL Mock draft toes the line between those two scenarios: New England indeed moves up in the first round but passes on a potential franchise quarterback to go a different route.

intro: Thursday kicks off the 2021 NFL Draft, providing hope to 32 teams, a dream job to 259 rookies and a weekend of sports entertainment to millions of fans. Teams will make decisions that influence the trajectory of their franchise years into the future, and each player’s career success will largely depend on where they’re drafted and how suitable that environment is to that unique individual. For the amount of time and effort spent studying prospects, even Indianapolis Colts general manager Chris Ballard will tell you it’s “not an exact science.”

intro: I finished it! Every year, I spend too much time worrying about something that, inevitably, will come undone an hour or two into the actual draft. But doing a mock, for me, is a great exercise to go through. And to steal my old coworker Daniel Jeremiah’s phraseology: I do this with my ears, not my eyes. I’m not scouting these guys. But over the last few weeks I’ve talked to a lot of people who have been doing that work for the last year, and as the draft’s drawn closer, with their help, I’ve worked on connecting players with teams.

description: 2021 Inside The Star Round 1 NFL Mock Draft ✭ : April 27, 2021 - As draft week heats up, so do the rumors surrounding everyone aside from Trevor Lawrence and Zach Wilson. Outside the top two players, there are so many
intro: As draft week heats up, so do the rumors surrounding everyone aside from Trevor Lawrence and Zach Wilson. Outside the top two players, there are so many ways the draft could fall. We’ll all be waiting with baited breath for our team’s opportunity to go on the clock. Remember the feeling of adulation when the Dallas Cowboys went on the clock at 17 and CeeDee Lamb was still available. I do. As a fan of the Oklahoma Sooners and the Dallas Cowboys I was so nervous. I wasn’t sure the Dallas Cowboys had it in them to take the best player available in CeeDee Lamb. And when they finally selected him, I couldn’t stop shaking.

description: Mock Draft: What A Sportsbook Version Would Look Like Based On Odds : Thousands of mock drafts are created each year using fits and other info. We're making our own mock draft based on odds from DraftKings.
intro: Each draft season, thousands of mock drafts hit the internet with a nearly infinite number of outcomes. Analysts try to combine tape with fit, paired with team rumors and scouting reports to craft the most accurate mock. Annually, the top analysts in the world shoot 50% and no better. Also each draft season, sportsbooks release hundreds of NFL Draft props– one of the world’s fastest-growing betting markets. With them comes player draft position over/unders, odds for specific teams to pick specific players, and odds one player will go before another.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft : The Gridiron Scholar’s 2021 NFL Mock Draft by John Laub fantasy football diehards
intro: or the sixth year, I am thrilled to publish my Mock Draft on FootballDiehards.com. Over the past seven months, I have studied the prospects and compiled my big board for all the positions. Before I sat down to make my selections, I reflected on the history of the NFL and the Draft. As a teenager in the Seventies, I grew up watching two dynamic Dynasties: Dallas and Pittsburgh. The franchises combined for nine Super Bowl appearances, including two against each other (X and XIII) and netted six Lombardi trophies. The organizations stood atop the NFL landscape.

description: PFT's one, and only, mock draft of the year - ProFootballTalk : ProFootballTalk 2021 NFL Mock Draft: PFT’s one, and only, mock draft of the year
intro: Mock drafts are like opinions and bungholes. Everybody has one and they all stink, except maybe your own. We used to do umpteen versions of mock drafts. That was before I developed a thorough and complete hatred of them. In recent years, we’ve shifted to a one-shot mock draft, with no concern for accuracy and no pride in authorship. Especially since I have now exported the assignment to a seasoned scout who currently is not employed by any team but whose credentials would not be questioned if his name were to be mentioned.

The last thing you need to know is two trades were completed during this mock draft. The team moving up paid a slight premium to do so, as is customary. Each trade involved the swapping of two picks for two picks. No team's pick total changed.

description: NFL mock draft: Saints trade WAY up for a QB and the Ravens get aggressive for a WR : Our latest mock with the real draft coming Thursday.
intro: The San Francisco 49ers may not be the only team that moves up for a quarterback. With the Atlanta Falcons, the Carolina Panthers and the New York Giants each reportedly expressing interest in moving down the board, we could see a team move up — and maybe way up — to secure their quarterback of the future. Because mock drafts shouldn’t be too serious and because I always want mine to be weird in one way or another, I figured I’d give it a try. This mock draft has plenty of fun and funky elements, but the centerpiece is a huge trade, with the New Orleans Saints moving into a spot where they can land their franchise quarterback, who (in my mind) would make them Super Bowl contenders in 2021.

description: 2021 NFL mock draft: Broncos trade up for Justin Fields, Bears trade for Jimmy Garoppolo : NFL Insider Matt Lombardo's 2021 NFL Mock Draft, complete with insight from various league sources on some of the top NFL Draft prospects.
intro: There is a very real possibility that as many as four quarterbacks are chosen in the first five picks and potentially five in the top-10, but there is as much uncertainty throughout this first round as can be remembered in recent history. Given that so many players opted out, and so few are eligible for this class, thanks to the NCAA’s eligibility rules following COVID-19 wreaking havoc on the 2020 season, there have already been several teams who have made trades to accumulate assets in next year’s draft, where many hope to find more certainty.

description: Ledyard's FINAL 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Multiple Trades, 5 QBs In Top 10 : Pewter Report's Jon Ledyard releases his final 2021 NFL mock draft, including multiple trades and 5 quarterbacks in the top 10.
intro: Three. More. Days. That’s how long we have until the real-life, actual 2021 NFL Draft occurs in Cleveland. The Bucs will pick 32nd in the draft order for the first time in franchise history on Thursday night. Until then, you’ll have to settle for my final mock draft, as I project all 32 picks with several trades. Five quarterbacks come off the board in the Top 10. Remember, this is a predictive mock draft, not necessarily what I would do. Armed with that knowledge, give me all of your hate.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft April 27 : The 2021 NFL Draft is this month and it is time to look at what may happen. Here is the 2021 NFL Mock Draft April 27.
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is this month and it is time to look at what may happen. Here is the 2021 NFL Mock Draft April 27.

description: Bucky Brooks 2021 NFL mock draft 3.0: 49ers pick Trey Lance; Najee Harris to Cardinals : In his final mock of the 2021 NFL Draft, Bucky Brooks has Alabama teammates Najee Harris and Mac Jones both going in the top 20, with the well-rounded RB off the board first at No. 16 overall.
intro: With the 2021 NFL Draft just days away, here's my third and final crack at projecting how Round 1 might play out on Thursday, April 29, in Cleveland.

description: Alex Katson and Josh Berg’s 7 Round Mock Draft : Alex Katson and Josh Berg team up for a full 7 round mock draft.
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is finally, mercifully, almost here. Everyone being stuck inside all year has launched discourse into a dark but also golden age: there are so many more bad takes, but occasionally some good ones too. All this back and forth is exactly why we haven’t written a mock draft yet this year. (Also, we are lazy and writing mock drafts is time consuming.) Instead, we have locked ourselves in separate, discourse-less bunkers, crafting our objectively correct opinions about every prospect in this year’s draft. Which leads us here. 7 rounds, 259 selections, only 7 trades because we gave up in the fourth round. Welcome to hell. A few notes before we get to the picks. We both drafted for 16 teams and rigged it so that we had as close to the same number of total picks as possible. Alex started with an extra pick since there’s an odd number. We also rigged it so that we could both draft for our favorite teams, which could be any of our respective 16, depending on how you feel about the outcome of the draft. Even though we both “controlled” half the league, every pick was made more or less collaboratively, except the especially bad ones, which were all the result of one of us going rouge.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Final first round predictions with trades : The 2021 NFL Draft is almost here and it will be an exciting night as the first round looks to be full of deals for positioning for certain players. - Page 2
intro: With the first round of the NFL Draft just days away, it is only fitting that a final mock draft is shown to the world. The entire internet will be littered with many different trials and tribulations of the first night but each will have its own uniqueness. This mock draft allowed for many teams to continue making trades even though many have already been done before the event began. It should be an exciting night for these franchises, fans, and prospects as the first round ticks down. Even after the first round concludes there will be many solid prospects left on the board for the taking. History tells us that many hall of fame and star-caliber players will be available even after the draft ends with the signing of undrafted free agents.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Ja'Marr Chase and Joe Burrow reunite : With the NFL Draft just days away, here is one of the last mock drafts of the year.
intro: It is finally draft week, and I cannot wait to hear commissioner Goodell announce that first pick. I believe this year’s draft will give us more trades, drama, and excitement than any other year due to so much uncertainty during this year’s pre-draft process. Here is my final shot at how things will shake out.

intro: Mac Jones was just 7 months old the last time an NFL Draft opened with quarterbacks going in each of the first three picks. That was 1999. And this is how Thursday night’s first round will unfold after that rare trifecta:

With all of that out the way, y’all get ready for three rounds of mediocre action.

description: Mike Tagliere’s Final 2021 NFL Mock Draft (4.0 – Three Rounds)
intro: This is it. No more hmming and hawing about what each team might do. It’s time to make my final predictions for what I believe each team will do in the 2021 NFL Draft. This is not a mock draft on what I would do; I want to be very clear on that. This is a compilation of what I’ve learned over the last few months, whether it be scouting prospects, listening to insiders, paying attention to the meetings that teams have had with prospects, and team needs.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft : Draft week is finally here and the entire staff at The Draft Network is releasing their own first-ro...
intro: Draft week is finally here and the entire staff at The Draft Network is releasing their own first-round predictive mock draft. Jake Arians, co-host of the TDN Fantasy Podcast, is dropping one too. Here's how the first round of the 2021 NFL Draft could play out on Thursday night.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft : The 2021 NFL Draft is almost here. This year's draft is going to be one of the most exciting in NFL ...
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is almost here. This year's draft is going to be one of the most exciting in NFL history. The process has truly been one-of-a-kind. I believe it is going to bring more shocks than people are expecting. With that said, here's my final predictive mock of the 2021 NFL Draft cycle. This is my best prediction as to how things will unfold Thursday night. Enjoy the mock, and enjoy draft weekend, friends.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft : Happy draft week, everyone! Some of you may recall, around this time last year, I released the ...
intro: Happy draft week, everyone! Some of you may recall, around this time last year, I released the very first “Marketing Mock.” Well, I decided to make it an annual occurrence. Kyle Crabbs, Joe Marino, and the rest of the TDN gang can stick to the predictive mock drafts. But I like doing something different—something off the beaten path. If you’re unfamiliar with my marketing mock, it’s exactly what you might anticipate. Instead of basing the picks solely off of team needs and positional value, these picks are rooted in the prospect’s marketing potential with the given team and the franchise’s ability to create revenue off of their brands as individuals.

description: 2021 NFL three-round mock draft : The NFL Draft begins on Thursday, with teams now putting the finishing touches on their draft boards. Here's a look at our final three-round NFL mock draft, as of April 26, 2021.
intro: The NFL Draft begins on Thursday, with teams now putting the finishing touches on their draft boards. Here's a look at our final three-round NFL mock draft, as of April 26, 2021.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Quarterbacks & O-Linemen Dominate Round 1 : Reinforcing the value of the position, five quarterbacks go in the top 10, while offensive linemen follow, in this 2021 NFL mock draft.
intro: Finally, It’s draft week—and there’s no better way to start the week than with a mock draft. I will humbly try to predict each one of the 32 first-round picks, with a particular focus on each team’s scheme fits and previous draft choices.  Of course, I will probably get it all wrong, but trying is the name of the game.

description: NFL Mock Draft presented by the Say It Again Network : The Say It Again Network decided to go through a first round pick and give our realistic picks of what we see in the mock draft.

description: Sam Teets 2021 NFL three-round predictive mock draft 6.0 : We're days away from the 2021 NFL Draft. As part of my final draft coverage, I've put together a three-round predictive mock.
intro: This is a predictive NFL mock draft and my final of the 2021 offseason. It’s been a fantastic pre-draft process. Part of me never wanted it to end because it’s a content goldmine that I enjoyed milking for the past few months. Please check back with The Grueling Truth and my Twitter account (@Sam_Teets33) for post-draft content throughout the remaining offseason. My final mock draft includes several predicted trades, but they mostly occur early in the first-round. I’ve included some other draft options below each first-round selection so readers can get a better understanding of my thought process. No extra writing accompanies the second and third-round selections.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Expert Picks & Analysis on Round 1 Prospects : Built by experts and based on the latest news, rumors, and stats, Sportsnaut's 2021 NFL mock draft does more than list names. Get the real Draft picture now.
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is drawing nearer, and as all the teams finalize their strategies and top prospects to target, it’s worth debating where the best players will go. Some of the key decisions at the top of the draft have already been made. Announcing those moves on draft night will be a mere formality. However, there’s still plenty of mystery to unravel beyond the obvious, no-brainer first-round picks. Let’s dive into our latest 2021 NFL mock draft, reflecting the first wave of NFL free agency and the two blockbuster trades that sent shockwaves through the 2021 NFL Draft. As you’ll see, scoring points is the priority among the teams picking early.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Bears trade up to capitalize on Justin Fields' fall, Patriots move up for QB : Bears look to fix past mistakes made at quarterback by moving up for Fields
intro: Mock drafts are hard. I mean, no, mocking players to certain teams on my laptop is not exactly breaking rocks. I don't deserve a medal. (Maybe Ryan Wilson does now that he's done 34 mocks this draft season.) But it's hard because you have to balance truth from fiction. I trust my sources and their honesty in a general sense, but we all have biases and loyalties. You also have to ignore what you believe about certain players or positions. I wouldn't take a running back in this year's class in the top 20, but you'll see one below. Some team in the top-12 may not care about DeVonta Smith's weight, but it is a factor to me.

intro: Lawrence is the best quarterback prospect since Andrew Luck in 2012. The Jaguars better not try to get too creative or over think this decision because he is a future hall of famer in my mind.

description: Silva: 2021 NFL Mock Draft 2.0
intro: This is my best guess as to how Thursday night’s first round will go based on intel I’ve gleaned and my knowledge of each team’s needs and targeted players. I’ll publish my final mock on Wednesday night.

description: Mock Draft 4.0: Mark Schofield's "Mock of Integrity" : The 2021 NFL draft is almost here, so it is time to go on the record. Mark Schofield has his final mock draft. Where does Mac Jones land?
intro: It is almost here. I am of course referring to the first 2022 mock draft of the next cycle, and I know you are all excited. On a more serious note, the 2021 NFL draft is almost here, with the Jacksonville Jaguars officially on the clock Thursday night to kick things off. As such, it is time for the “Mock of Integrity.” No more “What I would do?” mock drafts, but rather it is time to go on the record with what I think will happen Thursday night. We have some trades, a few quarterbacks sliding and yes, Mac Jones coming off the board in the first three selections.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Full Two-Round Projection : A full, two-round mock ahead of the 2021 NFL Draft.
intro: I'll keep this introduction quick: Here is my full two-round mock draft just days before the 2021 NFL Draft begins. A seven-round, Buccaneers-only mock draft will be published on AllBucs before the draft begins as well, and both their first and second round selections in this exercise will be reflected in that story. Explanations are offered for the top ten picks, for trades and other occasional picks, and of course, both of the Tampa Bay Buccaneers' selections. Two trades are included for quarterbacks, but otherwise this mock follows the draft order as currently set.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: First-Round Predictions (Updated) : Its officially draft week around the NFL and despite there still being a few days left until names are called out, most franchises arent sitting back...
intro: It's officially draft week around the NFL and despite there still being a few days left until names are called out, most franchises aren't sitting back and relaxing ahead of the first round. We've already seen an active trade market for picks and the wheeling and dealing is unlikely to stop once Commissioner Roger Goodell kicks things off in Cleveland. With all that in mind, here's a look at who could go where in the first round of the 2021 NFL Draft when factoring in a handful of potential moves up and down the board:

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Version 4.0 : Predictions on top 32 picks in the 2021 NFL Draft.
intro: There’s no secret with Jacksonville’s first pick. Lawrence is the best quarterback prospect in quite some time. He’s a prototypical NFL QB that possesses the ability to turn around any franchise within a couple of years. Jacksonville fired Doug Marrone after a 1-15 season, and they will be looking to set Urban Meyer up for a quick turnaround. After allowing Gardner Minshew, Mike Glennon, and Jake Luton to complete the tank in 2020, Lawrence is the clear top prospect that can help Jacksonville find their way back to the playoffs sooner rather than later.

description: Final 2021 Mock Draft : The 2021 NFL draft seems to hinge on one question: which team will reach out and grab Ohio State Buckeyes quarterback Justin Fields?
intro: We made it, everyone! It's officially NFL draft week (or the week before the draft since that's when I'm technically writing this article). Through the twists and turns of a college football season that almost didn't happen to not having a combine, this has been another unprecedented draft process. I don't have much else to say, except: may the NFL draft odds ever be in my favor here and in The Huddle Report Mock Draft Contest. A quick reminder on the ground rules I'm using for this mock draft. I'm not projecting any trades and I only take these things into account when formulating where I think a player is going to be selected and by whom:

description: The Tennessean's final 2021 NFL mock draft: Here's who Titans will pick | Estes : Adding a trio of trades to the mix in another mock draft before the fun finally starts for Tennessee Titans in Thursday's 2021 NFL Draft.
intro: OK, who's eager for another NFL mock draft? The pre-draft fatigue is growing on my end as Thursday night's first round approaches. It's time to get that train rolling, and once it does, most of the projections can probably be tossed. What I'm expecting, this draft more than most, is the unexpected. Some major surprises will be the result of this year's odd scouting process.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft for Football Morning In America - Peter King, NBC Sports : Peter King's Football Morning In America begins with the 2021 NFL Mock Draft, predicting all the picks for Thursday's first round.
intro: The best thing I can say about my mock draft: I am cautiously optimistic about hitting my first two picks. Before you see my mock draft and commence tomato-throwing at your screens—Penei Sewell, dropping to seven?! You are nuts!!!—a few headlines about what’s coming this week, prepping for the 8 p.m. Thursday kickoff to the three-day draft in downtown Cleveland: Another draft of hope, just a different kind. Last year, with Roger Goodell announcing picks from his basement and coaches and prospects all with in-home cams and Bill Belichick’s dog the hit of the weekend, the NFL put on a show that said, “Hey, this kind of sucks, but we’re plowing ahead and showing the country we can be business-approximately-as-usual in a pandemic.” This year, there will be Goodell on a downtown Cleveland stage, on the south shore of Lake Erie nestled between the Browns stadium and the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, announcing picks in front of two distinct fan groups: one fully vaccinated, closer together, with masks, and one non-vaccinated, spread apart, also with masks. Thirteen players on site walking the stage, 45 more (including top pick Trevor Lawrence) with home cams.

description: Final 2021 First Round NFL Mock Draft - Pats Buzz
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is just about here, which means it’s time for the final version of my 2021 Mock Draft. In my last mock, I didn’t try and project any trades, but for my last mock, I’ll predict a couple I think could take place. Keep in mind, this is what I think will happen and not necessarily what I would do as a GM. Without further ado, let’s get right into it!

description: Omar Kelly’s final first-round 2021 NFL mock draft: Who will Dolphins select at picks 6 and 18? : The Dolphins’ goal this offseason is to add playmakers to the offense, giving quarterback Tua Tagovailoa what he needs to succeed and in this 2021 first round NFL mock draft they would accomplish that goal.

While former Clemson quarterback Trevor Lawrence is the favorite to go No. 1, how will the rest of the first round shake out? USA TODAY Network experts have put on their general manager hats to make their picks. See who they took (and why).

description: 2021 Aftermath NFL Mock Draft: Patriots trade up for Mac Jones while the Dolphins grab two elite receivers : It's officially draft week! To kick off our week of draft content at the Aftermath, Brian Mandel, Jack Venezia and Matt Luppino joined me via Zoom to mock the first round of this year's draft. This was our sixth annual mock draft together. We each take eight picks and operate as the general manager for…
intro: It’s officially draft week! To kick off our week of draft content at the Aftermath, Brian Mandel, Jack Venezia and Matt Luppino joined me via Zoom to mock the first round of this year’s draft. This was our sixth annual mock draft together. We each take eight picks and operate as the general manager for that selection. This is what we would do if we were in charge on draft day, not what we think is going to happen. It’s a really fun exercise because we each have different priorities and draft boards. For the second year running, we also allowed for picks to be traded. We got a few really interesting swaps, which are as follows.

description: Nia's 2021 NFL Mock Draft 1.0 - Prime Time Sports Talk : With under a week until the 2021 NFL Draft, rumors and mock drafts are everywhere. Kamran Nia offers up his take with his Mock Draft 1.0.
intro: The NFL Draft is less than a week away, and teams are making their final pre-draft trades and finalizing who they plan to pick. Let’s take a look at who each team could draft with their first-round picks (no trades).

description: 2021 NFL mock draft: Micah Parsons joins former Penn State star, Jayson Oweh gets into first round, and more
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is just a couple of days away, so that means it’s time to take our best guess and how things will break down over the three-day, seven-round event later this week. There’s not much suspense at the top with Clemson quarterback Trevor Lawrence widely expected to go to the Jacksonville Jaguars with the No. 1 overall pick. But after that, there are plenty of questions remaining. Would the San Francisco 49ers really take Alabama quarterback Mac Jones at No. 3 overall? Will any defensive players go in the top 10? Could Penn State linebacker Micah Parsons be the first defensive player off the board? What about his teammate, pass rusher Jayson Oweh? How do teams see him?

description: Rhett Lewis 2021 NFL mock draft 1.0: Packers help Aaron Rodgers; Bills boost backfield : In his one and only mock draft of the season, Rhett Lewis predicts two of the league's top offenses will add firepower late in Round 1 of the 2021 NFL Draft.
intro: There are three basic ways to execute a mock draft. It's all about the mindset you have when making the picks. You can fill out the board based on: What you think will happen. What you think should happen. What you hear will happen via reporting and intel from sources. I've tried to combine all three mentalities for my annual -- my one and only -- mock draft of the year. So, before the 2021 NFL Draft kicks off with Round 1 this Thursday night in Cleveland, Ohio, let's give it a whirl!

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft : It’s draft week? It’s draft week! As we’ve lost track of days, hours even, we’ll now be able to full...
intro: It’s draft week? It’s draft week! As we’ve lost track of days, hours even, we’ll now be able to fully dive into the pageantry of the 2021 NFL Draft. If I’ve learned anything from decades watching the Seattle Seahawks through draft weekend, it’s that predicting anything—aside from the first hand full of picks, usually—is futile. This predictive mock is for the entire first round of the 2021 draft.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft : Rejoice and make merry: draft week is here!With draft week comes final mock drafts—though, of course...
intro: Rejoice and make merry: draft week is here! With draft week comes final mock drafts—though, of course, rarely on a Monday. This is the last mock I’ll write, but there may be changes made as we get closer to the day of. This mock, like over a hundred others, will be scored by The Huddle Report and must be submitted by Wednesday night. So if you really want to see the picks written in Sharpie, circle back on Thursday morning.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft : After all the pomp and circumstance of pro days, independent combines, medical checks, etc., followi...
intro: After all the pomp and circumstance of pro days, independent combines, medical checks, etc., following the most chaotic college football season ever, we're finally zeroing in on the 2021 NFL Draft. We're only double-digit hours away from finally finding out where the top college football prospects wind up in the NFL. As the clock ticks down to the Jacksonville Jaguars opening the 2021 draft, there's one final task for our staff here at The Draft Network to complete: a fully predictive mock draft. The time for 'what we would do' or 'what teams should do' is over. It's now all about what we think is most likely to happen on night one of the draft.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft : Ladies and gentlemen, we’ve made it. From the “way too early mocks” back in April of last year, to t...
intro: Ladies and gentlemen, we’ve made it. From the “way too early mocks” back in April of last year, to the COVID-19 rattled 2020 season, we’ve come full circle. Draft week is HERE.  You’ve seen the abundance of mocks from our analysts over the last couple of months here at The Draft Network, but this is my one and final mock I’ll have for you this time around. From what each team could value most, to what I’ve been hearing internally around the league, this mock trends away from any you’ve probably seen recently. I held off on trades, instead, I kept the draft in its purest form, offering a glimpse into how Thursday night could play out if each team were to stay exactly where they are, and who they would select based on team needs.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft : Well, folks, this is it. The last mock draft you’ll ever read.OK, not even I believe that.But this i...
intro: Well, folks, this is it. The last mock draft you’ll ever read. OK, not even I believe that. But this is the end of the road for my mock drafting. As it is draft week, this is when all the research, the sources, the conversation, the hints, the clues, and the assessments meet. I have very much enjoyed the steps of this 2021 draft cycle with you all, and I hope that along the way you’ve enjoyed it, too. Thank you all for the reads, the downloads, the tweets, the messages, the back-and-forths. I have learned so much about the pulse of each fan base throughout the course of this draft season -- an aspect of this job I truly love. But it’s time to give it a wrap and a bow.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: 49ers give the nod to Justin Fields, Falcons gift Matt Ryan a generational weapon : 2021 NFL Mock Draft: 49ers give the nod to Justin Fields, Falcons give Matt Ryan a generational weapon
intro: You can almost smell it. It's nearly here. The 2021 NFL Draft is only days away and all 32 teams have, at this point, finalized their big boards and are readying to pull their respective triggers on April 29 in Cleveland. There won't be any surprise with the No. 1 overall pick, but don't use a pen when writing down what you think will happen thereafter. For as is usually the case going into the draft, not everything you hear is true, and there are bound to be surprises here and there. In our latest first-round mock, that is precisely what occurred, and the early run on quarterbacks helped shape the remainder of the round -- as did what non-QBs got selected and where. Some teams walked away with a huge smile knowing their patience paid off in not striking a trade, while others were forced to think a bit harder after failing to find a partner to move up.

description: 2021 NFL mock draft: What each team should do in the first round : No predictions in this 2021 NFL mock draft. Here, we are using team needs and player evaluations to determine what each team should do, not what they will.
intro: Another day, another 2021 NFL mock draft. Everybody in the industry is making their final predictions ahead of Thursday’s 2021 NFL Draft and we seem to have an idea of what is going to happen at the top. While there is always plenty of uncertainty, we at least have a solid idea of what the first few teams will be looking to do at the top of the draft. However, none of that matters in this 2021 NFL mock draft. We are going to do things a bit differently here. In this 2021 NFL mock draft, I am not making any predictions. Instead, I am going to make picks for all 32 selections in the first round based on who I believe would be the best fit for the team. Team needs will be taken into account but this 2021 NFL mock draft will primarily be based on my evaluations on this draft class. Also, we are going to leave trades completely out of this exercise.

description: Full seven-round NFL mock draft (with trades!) with a Cowboys focus : With a few days until the draft, who will the Cowboys take?
intro: It’s almost here; the 2021 NFL draft is only a few short days away. They’ve enjoyed a promising offseason so far, with Dak Prescott being locked up on a long-term contract and several key additions on defense via free agency. Now comes the draft. The hype around the tenth pick involves the debate between Patrick Surtain II and Jaycee Horn, while others are praying Kyle Pitts or Penei Sewell falls that far. Either way, this draft is likely to be full of surprises all around. This mock draft, which includes mock trades in the first round, has plenty of surprises of its own as well.

description: 2021 3-round NFL mock draft: Washington Football Team adds a QB : The 2021 NFL Draft is less than a week away, and the Washington Football Team is on red alert. This 3-round NFL mock predicts the picks for all 32 teams. - Page 2

I apologize in advance for the strain clicking the “next” button will have on your index finger. However, I assure you: The carpel tunnel syndrome that set in from writing this is probably worse. Let’s get into the picks.

Both the Denver Broncos and Chicago Bears decide to throw their chips into the middle of the table and land a player they can believe in. With all that being said, let’s check out this three-round 2021 NFL Mock Draft with trade predictions.

intro: Having worked on the 2021 NFL Mock Draft consistently since last May, my confidence level over the prospects and team needs is sky-high. Two things are becoming clear in the stretch run. And secondly, cornerbacks are rising, which shouldn’t surprise anybody in the new pass-first NFL. Let’s dive into my latest mock draft.

Lack of game film and measurables present a unique challenge for teams performing due diligence on prospects. Resultantly, predicting the first round picks for each team is a bit esoteric. I surveyed several recent mock drafts to identify positional trends and researched team needs accordingly to create something close to realistic result. Each pick will assess team needs and the best prospects available, but not all are necessarily the right picks. Much like the actual NFL draft.

intro: With roughly one week to go until the start of the 2021 NFL Draft in Cleveland, the only certainty that lays ahead is that we will most certainly see five quarterbacks taken in the first round, which would be the ninth time since the merger that four or more passers were taken in the first round. That could very well include each of the top three teams in the first round taking a quarterback in hopes that they have their No. 1 man under center for the foreseeable future.

description: Mock Draft 2.0 - 2 rounds and more | WIBW News Now : Brendan Dzwierzynski's 2nd 2021 NFL mock draft features 2 rounds, plus alternate scenarios for every pick.
intro: A staple of our yearly mock draft collection is here, the two-round mock with alternate picks. It’s a simple setup: we have two rounds of picks, with two other options listed at each spot. This could include different players or a trade scenario. This year’s No. 1 pick is as obvious as it gets (hint: it’s Trevor Lawrence to the Jacksonville Jaguars), but this year’s draft should be as compelling as any one in recent memory given the fact there was no combine and subsequently less scouting groupthink, and pro day testing numbers are questionable at best.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft 6.0 From Ryan McCrystal | Sharp Football : 2021 NFL Mock Draft 6.0 that still features Mac Jones at No. 3 and a trade up for Justin Fields.
intro: With less than two weeks before draft night, here’s the final mock before the final mock.  In this version, I’ve included a trade for the first time. I typically prefer to avoid trades in mock drafts, but it’s becoming increasingly likely that either the quarterbacks will fall much further than expected or someone is going to make a move into the top 10.

description: Mock Draft 3.0: 49ers Pick Justin Fields; Tackles Go No. 4 and 5 : Two of the five top QB prospects slide to the end of the top 10, as several teams fill other needs with offensive linemen and playmakers.
intro: Trevor Lawrence has been penciled in as the No. 1 pick in this draft for two years, and I consider it refreshing that he and the Jaguars haven’t found it necessary to send faux smoke screens to stoke false intrigue. But there is plenty of real intrigue about the rest of the QB class after Lawrence and Zach Wilson: Whom did Kyle Shanahan trade up for? Will the Patriots make a move for a QB and who will it be? Will five QBs be drafted in the top 10, which didn’t even happen in the Great Quarterback Boom of 1983? This year’s draft class also includes a novel factor in evaluations, either in evaluating film from an atypical 2020 college football season or in assessing players who opted out last year as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic. But as Bill Belichick told reporters last week, “It’s maybe a little bit less information than we normally have, but all teams are working with the same general information.” That characterization is a bit too on the nose for us hapless mock drafters—in any year. Here’s this year’s try.

description: 2021 NFL mock draft: What might happen in first round if Cowboys, Broncos trade up? : Quarterbacks are the focal point of the NFL draft, but a tight end could become the talk of the first round if the Cowboys trade up for Kyle Pitts.
intro: The late, great Tom Petty summed it up pretty well 40 years ago this month when we first heard him sing, 'The waiting is the hardest part.' Kind of encapsulates where we are in the NFL's pre-draft season with the main event still 10 days away. But before that happens, Trey Lance will throw again for scouts, some medical evaluations will continue to await resolution – all as the 32 teams finalize their boards – and the annual, late-stage smokescreens will begin to billow. One recent rumor that's gained traction stems from an ESPN report claiming that Cowboys owner Jerry Jones is 'infatuated' with uber-talented Florida tight end Kyle Pitts. Is that true? Is it counterintelligence? Is it legit even if Jones doesn't plan a bold move up from No. 10? Hard to know.

description: Sam Farmer's NFL mock draft 2.0: Predicting QB picks as easy as one, two, three  : Quarterbacks are expected to be the first three picks of the NFL draft for the first time since 1999 with Clemson's Trevor Lawrence taken No. 1 overall.

In the case of The Times’ mock draft 2.0, that means quarterbacks going 1-2-3 for the first time since 1999 when the first three selections were Tim Couch, Donovan McNabb and Akili Smith. It also means three Alabama players chosen among the top 10, and a total of six in the first round. And it projects three running backs going in the opening round, something that has happened just twice in the last 10 years, as teams increasingly have stopped using first-round picks on that position.

intro: We're closing in on the 2021 NFL Draft, which officially kicks off on April 29. As we await Roger Goodell stepping onto the podium and putting the Jacksonville Jaguars on the clock with that No. 1 overall pick, why not take a stab at what may be in store for us? The powers that be here at CBS Sports asked for a mock draft from yours truly and -- being the company man I am -- I delivered. By now, we all have a good sense of what's to come in the top few selections, but things can go off the rails rather quickly after that. In this mock, I have just one trade going down on draft night, which involves Bill Belichick and the New England Patriots jumping up a few spots to bring in a quarterback they can develop behind Cam Newton. However, that deal comes outside of the top 10, where four other quarterbacks had already come off the board.

description: 2021 NFL mock draft: Eagles end up in tricky situation : Reuben Frank, Dave Zangaro, Barrett Brooks and Ray Didinger got together for a first-round NFL mock draft.
intro: With Danny Pommells hosting a special edition of the Eagle Eye podcast, Reuben Frank, Dave Zangaro, Barrett Brooks and Ray Didinger went through all 32 first-round picks. The order was chosen at random and repeated throughout the draft. No trades were allowed. Listen to the entire podcast here.

description: NFL mock draft: Colts select offensive tackle in full Round 1 mock : This Indianapolis Colts mock draft has Chris Ballard adding a potential starter on the offensive line for the next decade with pick No. 21
intro: The Indianapolis Colts will be one of the most interesting teams to watch in the 2021 NFL Draft, as they have the ability to go in one of several different directions with the No. 21 pick in this loaded draft class. The Colts need replacements at wide receiver and cornerback, but the exceptional talent at tight end, offensive tackle, and safety could help Chris Ballard shake up the latter half of the draft. While the top of the draft might be somewhat easy to predict, the Colts could completely turn the latter half of the draft on its head by making one unusual pick.

description: 4-Round NFL Mock Draft 2021: History made as quarterbacks dominate first round | PFN : With less than two weeks until the start of the 2021 NFL Draft, how does our final 4-Round Mock Draft see events unfolding in Cleveland?
intro: With the craziness of free agency behind us, NFL teams are finalizing their plans for the 2021 NFL Draft. We’ve never had a clearer idea of what teams may need than we have right now. With less than two weeks to go until the NFL’s annual selection event, it’s time for my final 2021 4-Round NFL Mock Draft!

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Patriots and Broncos trade up for their quarterbacks, Bengals reunite Burrow with Chase : Mac Jones falls on draft day
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is less than two weeks away, and mock draft season has certainly ramped up. The first two picks appear to be locked in, but more questions surround the San Francisco 49ers at No. 3 overall. They paid a king's ransom to trade up into the top three, and while we know they will likely take a quarterback, who will it be? Original reports seemed to indicate Kyle Shanahan was interested in Mac Jones out of Alabama, but could that have changed? What happens at that No. 3 spot could affect what happens in the first round at large. Depending on which quarterback is selected by the 49ers, it could make it more likely or unlikely that the Atlanta Falcons trade down from No. 4 overall. While the quarterback scenarios in the first round are intriguing, there is plenty of talent available in this class. In what seems like every position group, there are multiple players who are capable of coming in and contributing immediately. Now, it's time to release my mock draft.

description: David Dorey's 2021 NFL mock draft 3.0 : Dorey forecasts a quarterback-heavy opening to the draft.
intro: David Dorey’s third mock draft of 2021 is now out. Sound off on social media and let us know what you think. We’ll be unveiling more mock drafts than ever before this year, so be sure to check back with our 2021 NFL Draft Central page to keep track of all of our offerings.

description: Walker Kelly’s Final 2021 Mock Draft : (ANDREW FERGUSON / GETTY IMAGES) For my final mock of the 2021 NFL Draft season, I have a bit of a twist. Instead of writing a little blurb about each prospect that’s pretty similar to something you could read elsewhere, I’m going to give my grade for each player, a grade for each pick, and…
intro: For my final mock of the 2021 NFL Draft season, I have a bit of a twist. Instead of writing a little blurb about each prospect that’s pretty similar to something you could read elsewhere, I’m going to give my grade for each player, a grade for each pick, and who I would pick instead for every pick I don’t grade as an A. Thank you for reading!

description: NFLHeads release 2021 NFL Mock Draft 3.0 | 49ers select a QB at 3 : Who will the San Francisco 49ers draft? NFLHeads writers break down their Mock Draft and give the 49ers a much needed prospect, but who?
intro: Who will the San Francisco 49ers draft? NFLHeads writers break down their Mock Draft and give the 49ers a much needed prospect, but is it who you expected?

description: 32 TEAM 2021 NFL MOCK DRAFT ROUNDS 1 AND 2 - Cowboys Coffee Talk : The following is a 2021 32 team NFL mock draft for rounds one and two with a main focus on the Dallas Cowboys.
intro: The following 2021 NFL mock draft is consistent with my latest Dallas Cowboys 7 round mock draft with the Dallas Cowboys drafting a cornerback to pair with second year standout Trevon Diggs. In this 2 round mock, the Cowboys address their secondary with a couple of popular picks Other things of note are 4 quarterbacks taken in the top 10, 9 consecutive offensive players selected at the top of the draft, and four linebackers taken in the first round. There are some notable exclusions that may raise some eyebrows as well as some surprise selections but there are no trades in this Mock Draft. With no further adieu, here is the Cowboys Coffee Talk, 2 round, 2021, 32 team NFL mock draft.

description: Three-round NFL Mock Draft: Four Wolverines come off the board : The first 105 picks of the draft are covered here.
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is two weeks away, meaning mock draft season is in full frenzy. The Michigan Wolverines should have a handful of players selected on the first two nights of the draft with a player that seemingly is close to a first-round lock in defensive end Kwity Paye. Michigan fans make up a number of NFL fanbases given the program’s national reach, so we extended this edition to three rounds so everyone is covered. There are not any trades this time around. Simply the best pick your team could make in the slot it current slot it holds.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Patriots get Trevor Lawrence in all-trades, chaos edition : A new 2021 NFL mock draft where chaos reigns and every first-round pick gets traded -- starting with the Patriots getting Trevor Lawrence.
intro: It wouldn’t be far from the truth to say that the 2021 NFL Draft has already turned a bit chaotic in the first round. From the 49ers trading up to No. 3, the Dolphins then trading back up to No. 6 so the Eagles could move down, to the other first-round picks that had previously been dealt, all the way to the trades that could still be in the cards, it’s been a wild ride already. And we’re still two weeks away from the draft actually beginning on Thursday, April 29. But instead of marveling at the chaos (and admittedly enjoying it), what if we fully embraced it?

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft Picks 1-16 -
intro: This is one of the first productions of the 2021 Mock draft from The Draft Zone. This mock draft has been some of the hardest to evaluate, primarily due to no combine, but just determining what teams are interested in and the trades and what they truly mean.  Enjoy the madness, I am confident I got the first pick correct.

description:
2021 NFL Mock Draft: Nogle 2.0 sees Dolphins lose out on two top targets : Why do we add a decimal point to our mock draft numbers? Is there going to be a 2.1 version? I do not plan to make small adjustments. I guess it is just something we do.
Anyway, after that rambling...
intro: Why do we add a decimal point to our mock draft numbers? Is there going to be a 2.1 version? I do not plan to make small adjustments. I guess it is just something we do. Anyway, after that rambling introduction, it has been about two weeks since I put together my first 2021 NFL Mock Draft. We are just over two weeks away from the 2021 NFL Draft, so it seems like a perfect time for me to take another look at the first round and see what I think might happen on April 29.

description: Matt Williamson's First Round 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Five QBs drafted in the top 10? : Former scout and ESPN analyst Matt Williamson shares his latest 2021 First Round NFL Mock Draft for PFN. Will five QBs go in the top 10?
intro: (Editor’s Note) After the initial wave of free agency and ahead of the 2021 NFL season, former NFL scout and ESPN analyst Matt Williamson shares his latest 2021 First Round NFL Mock Draft. With just a few weeks until the NFL Draft, how does Williamson see the first round shaping up?

description: Raiders fill big offensive need in Review-Journal mock draft : This mock draft has the Raiders taking Oklahoma State offensive tackle Tevin Jenkins with the 17th pick in the first round.
intro: Draft day is drawing closer as NFL teams shore up their draft boards. The countdown is on to find out how much of the pre-draft information is true and how much is a smoke screen. After having the Raiders select Alabama defensive tackle Christian Barmore with their 17th pick in the first round, this new version of the Review-Journal’s mock draft has them taking Oklahoma State offensive tackle Tevin Jenkins with that selection.

description: Fanelli's 2021 NFL Mock Draft 3.0 - Prime Time Sports Talk
intro: In two short weeks, the 2021 NFL Draft will finally be here. After months of scouting reports and mock drafts, the real thing will have arrived. However, these next two weeks are going to feel like two months. Until the draft is here, the best we can do is read mock drafts. In my third mock draft of the year, this one will cover the first three rounds and include 17 trades. Let’s dive in!

description: 4th Annual, 32-Person Mock NFL Draft : It's time for our favorite mock draft of the NFL offseason as we're joined by 32 fellow writers, podcasters and analysts!
intro: For the fourth consecutive year we’ve gathered 32 of our favorite NFL Draft writers, podcasters and analysts for our annual 32-person, first-round mock draft. We’ve linked you to the Twitter page of each selector, so feel free to let them know what you think about their pick, or leave us your thoughts in the comments section below. Highlighted players have 2021 NFL Draft Player Profiles so be sure to check those out as we break down the players as both NFL Draft prospects and how they’ll factor into dynasty league rookie drafts.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: San Francisco 49ers Snag Mac Jones at No. 3, Shaking Things Up : Our latest 2021 NFL mock draft, which sees the 49ers taking Mac Jones and the Falcons snagging Kyle Pitts.
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is little more than two weeks away and all attention has turned to lining up prospects as teams jockey for position to select them. With that in mind, here's our latest NFL mock draft. (One note, this is a no-trades mock).

description: Maurice Jones-Drew 2021 NFL mock draft 2.0: Two trades shake up top 10 : In his second mock of the 2021 NFL Draft, Maurice-Jones Drew projects a pair of quarterback-fueled trades in the top 10. Plus, two running backs come off the board in Round 1.
intro: With the 2021 NFL Draft kicking off at the end of this month, here's my second projection of the first round. And this time around, I have two trades shaking up the top 10.

description: NFL Mock Draft 4.0: Unexpected Pick For SF, Sewell Falls Out of Top 10 (Picks 1-16) – OutKick : NFL Mock Draft 4.0: Picks 17-32 If you can believe it, we are only 2.5 weeks away from the 2021 NFL draft, which starts at 8 p.m. ET on Thursday, April
intro: If you can believe it, we are only 2.5 weeks away from the 2021 NFL draft, which starts at 8 p.m. ET on Thursday, April 29. As we get closer, more information is being revealed on where certain guys are projected to go. Like I always say, draft season is lying season, so you have to be good at sifting through the noise to find what’s real. And the worst part is that it’s only going to get worse as we inch closer, so we have to prepare ourselves. Since my last mock, a lot has happened. The San Francisco 49ers traded up to No. 3, while the Dolphins moved down — and then back up — landing at No. 6 overall. The belief is that Kyle Shanahan wants a quarterback, but which one will it be?

description: NFL Mock Draft Monday: San Francisco 49ers get their quarterback of the future : 2021 NFL four-round mock draft. Who does your favorite team draft?
intro: 2021 NFL four-round mock draft. Who does your favorite team draft?

description: NFL Mock Draft 2021: Patriots, Washington trade up in 3-round mock : The New England Patriots and Washington Football Team get bold and move up for QBs in this latest 2021 NFL mock draft projection.
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is less than three weeks away, which means that 2021 NFL Mock Draft season is nearly complete. For the time being, we get to have fun with hypothetical scenarios. What teams are going to be aggressive? Which teams are going to jump into the fray for quarterbacks in this particular class? There are a number of trades that could happen and a couple that we will explore in this 2021 NFL mock draft scenario. The primary positions in focus for trade, as usual, will be at the quarterback position, but teams could get aggressive for offensive tackles or cornerbacks in this class as well.

description: 2021 NFL Draft: Las Vegas Raiders fill void on offense in full first-round mock : The Las Vegas Raiders hold the No. 17 overall pick in the 2021 NFL Draft and they find a potential All-Pro in this full first-round mock. - Page 2
intro: The 2021 NFL Draft is just a few weeks away and the subterfuge and smoke screens are in full effect. Aside from the first pick of the draft which is as close to a certainty as you can get, the rest of the draft, including when the Las Vegas Raiders are on the clock, should have plenty of movers and shakers especially in the top half of the first round. There are 4-5 quarterbacks that many believe will be off the board in the top-10 alone and we could see a historic run of quarterbacks taken with the first four picks of the draft if things fall a certain way. We have teams that have traded up and down then back up in the top-10 which could make things very interesting.

description: 2021 NFL Mock Draft: New England Patriots trade up for QB Mac Jones, Cincinnati Bengals take WR Ja'Marr Chase | NFL Draft | PFF : Both the New England Patriots and Denver Broncos trade up for their presumed quarterbacks of the future in Sam Monson's latest mock draft.
intro: A few weeks removed from a few blockbuster trades involving first-round draft picks, we have now all grown comfortable with the world where the San Francisco 49ers and Kyle Shanahan own the No. 3 overall selection — the first spot in the 2021 NFL Draft where some uncertainty exists. We assume those first two picks are largely set in stone and that the real chaos unfolds from that point onward. So let’s put together a potential scenario in the form of my first 2021 NFL mock draft.

description: Alabama Football: Mac rises in 2021 NFL Mock Draft 4.0 : Alabama football fans want to know where their favorite players will go in the 2021 NFL Draft. This mock draft has seven going in the first round.
intro: We are less than three weeks away from the 2021 NFL Draft, and Alabama football players still don’t know where they will be drafted. However, we have more data as the draft approaches, so we can do 2021 NFL mock drafts with more confidence. We know what to expect with at least the first two picks, but it seems that the top three are currently locked in. Beyond that, some teams have tipped their hand and shown where they are leaning.

description: 2021 First-Round NFL Mock Draft: Updated Picks After Pro Days : We're down to about a fortnight now.   It feels as though it was just yesterday when the Tampa Bay Buccaneers were whipping the Vince Lombardi Trophy around above the deep waters of ...
intro: We're down to about a fortnight now. It feels as though it was just yesterday when the Tampa Bay Buccaneers were whipping the Vince Lombardi Trophy around above the deep waters of the Hillsborough River, but that's ancient history in terms of the NFL offseason, and free agency is already a memory. We're into the final countdown for the 2021 draft. And with basically the entire predraft process complete, we're able to mock the draft with an increasingly clear picture. Here's our latest stab at it.

description: Valdovinos' 3-Round 2021 NFL Mock Draft: Who moves up for Fields? : Matt Valdovinos' final 3-Round 2021 NFL Mock Draft -- who does your team take? How will the draft break down after QBs go 1-2-3?
intro: As the final Thursday in April beckons us from afar, we still lay at the mercy of Father Time. Each day that we move closer to the 2021 NFL Draft, the time seems to move slower. However, with each bit of information we get, the picture gets clearer. The first two picks are all but made, and the 49ers are guaranteed to take a quarterback at No. 3 — just a matter of which one. The Atlanta Falcons are the most pivotal selection this year. They’ll determine how the rest of the draft goes down. How does your team do in this 3-Round 2021 NFL Mock Draft?

description: 3-round mock draft 3.0 — Liam loves football :

2021 NFL Mock Draft: Who is willing to pay the Atlanta Falcons? : Following a wave of pro days in place of a conventional combine, how has the 2021 NFL Draft board shaken out following a collection of impressive workouts?
intro: With the conventional NFL combine thrown to the wayside in favor of a flurry of individual pro days for some of the 2021 NFL Draft’s top prospects due to such unique circumstances, the 2021 NFL Draft has seen a gradual shift in which prospects look to be first-round favorites. With names swapping throughout the back half of the first round, who finds their way into day one in this NFL mock draft? Even within the top half of the first round of the 2021 NFL Draft, a pair of trades from the Miami Dolphins saw a shuffle in draft selections that saw the San Francisco 49ers commit to selecting a rookie quarterback and the Philadelphia Eagles slide outside of position for one of the top three wide receivers of the class.
